0.ai_notes: 'Case': a specific example or situation being studied or discussed.
0.remarks: 'Case': 被研究或讨论的具体案例或情况。

1.ai_notes: 'G.N.': initials used here as a placeholder name for a person.
1.remarks: 'G.N.': 此处用作人名的首字母缩写占位符。

2.original: Mr. G.N. is now in his late thirties, is well-educated, works as an accountant,
2.b1: G.N. is in his late thirties, well-educated, and works as an accountant.
2.cn: G.N.现在三十多岁，受过良好教育，是一名会计。
2.ai_notes: 'late thirties': the years between 37 and 39 of a person's age. 'well-educated': having a high level of schooling or academic training.
2.remarks:
'late thirties': 三十七到三十九岁之间。
'well-educated': 受过良好教育的，具备高水平学术训练的。

3.b1: He lives by himself in Chicago.
3.ai_notes: 'lives alone': to live by oneself without roommates or family.
3.remarks: 'lives alone': 独自居住，没有室友或家人。

4.original: G.N.'s big desire is to get married.
4.b1: G.N. really wants to get married.
4.ai_notes: 'big desire': a very strong wish or ambition.
4.remarks: 'big desire': 强烈的愿望或抱负。

5.original: He wants love, companionship, a home, children, the works.
5.b1: He wants love, a partner, a home, and children.
5.cn: 他想要爱情、伴侣、家庭和孩子，想要这一切。
5.ai_notes: 'the works': an idiom meaning everything possible or everything that is usually included in a set.
5.remarks: 'the works': 习语，意为一切可能的事物或通常包含的所有东西。

6.original: G.N. has been close to marriage.
6.b1: G.N. has almost gotten married before.
6.ai_notes: 'close to marriage': to be in a relationship where marriage is being seriously considered or planned.
6.remarks: 'close to marriage': 处于一段正在认真考虑或计划结婚的关系中。

7.original: Once he was only one day away.
7.b1: Once, he was only one day away from it.
7.cn: 有一次，他离结婚只差一天。
7.ai_notes: 'one day away': very close to a specific event or deadline.
7.remarks: 'one day away': 距离某个特定事件或截止日期非常近。

8.original: But each time he has been near marriage, he discovers something wrong with the girl he's about to marry.
8.b1: But every time he gets close, he finds a problem with the woman.
8.cn: 但每次快结婚时，他都会发现对方身上有什么问题。
8.ai_notes: 'discovers something wrong': to find a flaw or a reason to doubt someone.
8.remarks: 'discovers something wrong': 发现瑕疵或怀疑某人的理由。

9.original: Just in time before I made an awful mistake.
9.b1: He thinks he avoided a big mistake just in time.
9.cn: 他觉得自己及时避免了一个严重的错误。
9.ai_notes: 'just in time': at the last possible moment before something bad happens. 'awful mistake': a very serious or regrettable error.
9.remarks:
'just in time': 在坏事发生前的最后一刻。
'awful mistake': 非常严重或令人遗憾的错误。

10.b1: One story is a good example.
10.ai_notes: 'stands out': to be very noticeable or clearly better/different than others.
10.remarks: 'stands out': 非常显眼，或明显优于/不同于他人。

11.original: Two years ago, Jian thought he finally had met just the right girl.
11.b1: Two years ago, Jian thought he finally met the right girl.
11.cn: 两年前，Jian以为他终于遇到了对的人。
11.ai_notes: 'just the right girl': the perfect person for a specific role or relationship.
11.remarks: 'just the right girl': 适合特定角色或关系的完美人选。

12.b1: She was pretty, nice, and smart.
12.cn: 她很有魅力，性格好，也很聪明。
12.ai_notes: 'pleasant': friendly, kind, and easy to get along with.
12.remarks: 'pleasant': 友好的，和蔼的，容易相处的。

13.original: But Jian had to be absolutely sure that marriage was the right thing.
13.b1: But Jian needed to be completely sure about the marriage.
13.cn: 但Jian必须百分之百确定这段婚姻是正确的。
13.ai_notes: 'absolutely sure': completely certain; having no doubt at all.
13.remarks: 'absolutely sure': 完全确定，毫无疑问。

14.original: As they were discussing marriage plans one evening, the future Mrs. Jian made a few remarks that bothered Jian.
14.b1: While they were planning the wedding, she said some things that worried him.
14.cn: 一天晚上，当他们讨论婚礼计划时，未来的Jian太太说了一些让Jian感到困扰的话。
14.ai_notes: 'future Mrs. Jian': a way to refer to a woman who is expected to become someone's wife. 'remarks': comments or things said.
14.remarks:
'future Mrs. Jian': 指代预期将成为某人妻子的女性。
'remarks': 评论或所说的话。

15.original: So, to make certain he was marrying the right girl, G.N. drew up a four-page document of stipulations she was to agree to before they got married.
15.b1: To be safe, G.N. wrote a four-page contract that she had to sign.
15.cn: 为了确保万无一失，G.N.起草了一份四页纸的协议，要求她在婚前签字同意。
15.ai_notes: 'drew up': to prepare or write a formal document, like a contract. 'stipulations': specific requirements or conditions that must be met.
15.remarks:
'drew up': 起草或书写正式文件，如合同。
'stipulations': 必须满足的具体要求或条件。

16.original: The document, neatly typed and looking very legal, covered every segment of living G.N. could think of.
16.b1: The document looked professional and covered every part of their life.
16.cn: 这份文件打印得很整齐，看起来很正式，涵盖了G.N.能想到的生活的方方面面。
16.ai_notes: 'neatly typed': written clearly using a computer or typewriter. 'legal': appearing official and binding under the law.
16.remarks:
'neatly typed': 用电脑或打字机清晰地打印出来。
'legal': 在法律上看起来正式且具有约束力。

17.original: There was one section on religion, what church they would go to, how often they would attend,
17.b1: It included rules about religion, which church to attend, and how often.
17.cn: 其中一部分关于宗教，规定了他们去哪家教堂，以及去多久。
17.ai_notes: 'section': a distinct part of a larger document or book.
17.remarks: 'section': 大型文档或书籍中的独立部分。

18.b1: It also said how much money they should give to the church.
18.cn: 还规定了他们要捐多少钱。
18.ai_notes: 'donate': to give money or goods to an organization, often for charity or religious purposes.
18.remarks: 'donate': 向组织捐赠金钱或物品，通常用于慈善或宗教目的。

19.original: Another section covered children, how many and when.
19.b1: Another part was about children, including how many and when to have them.
19.cn: 另一部分关于孩子，规定了生几个以及何时生。
19.ai_notes: 'in detail': including all the small, specific points.
19.remarks: 'in detail': 包含所有细小的、具体的点。

20.original: In detail, G.N. outlined the kind of friends they would have, his future wife's employment status,
20.b1: G.N. also wrote down details about their friends and her job.
20.cn: G.N.详细列出了他们会结交什么样的朋友，以及他未来妻子的工作状态。
20.ai_notes: 'outlined': to give a summary or a list of the main points. 'employment status': whether someone has a job and what kind of job it is.
20.remarks:
'outlined': 给出主要观点的摘要或列表。
'employment status': 某人是否有工作以及工作的类型。

21.original: where they would live, how their income would be spent.
21.b1: He also decided where they would live and how to spend their money.
21.cn: 还规定了他们住哪里，以及如何支配收入。
21.ai_notes: 'income': the money someone earns from work or investments.
21.remarks: 'income': 某人通过工作或投资赚取的金钱。

22.original: To finish the document, G.N. devoted half a page to listing specific habits the girl must break or must acquire.
22.b1: Finally, he listed habits she needed to change or start.
22.cn: 最后，G.N.用了半页纸列出了她必须改掉或养成的具体习惯。
22.ai_notes: 'devoted': to give a large amount of time or space to a specific task. 'acquire': to gain or develop a new habit or skill.
22.remarks:
'devoted': 将大量时间或空间投入到某项特定任务中。
'acquire': 获得或养成一种新习惯或技能。

23.original: This covered such habits as smoking, drinking, makeup, entertainment, and so on.
23.b1: This included smoking, drinking, makeup, and hobbies.
23.cn: 这涵盖了吸烟、饮酒、化妆、娱乐等习惯。
23.ai_notes: 'and so on': a phrase used to indicate that there are more similar items not mentioned.
23.remarks: 'and so on': 用于表示还有更多未提及的类似项目。

24.original: When Jian's prospective bride reviewed his ultimatum, she did what you would expect.
24.b1: When she read his list, she did what you would expect.
24.cn: 当Jian的未婚妻看完这份最后通牒时，她做出了你预料之中的反应。
24.ai_notes: 'prospective bride': a woman who is expected to become a bride soon. 'ultimatum': a final demand or statement of terms, where rejection will result in a break-up.
24.remarks:
'prospective bride': 预期即将成为新娘的女性。
'ultimatum': 最后通牒，即拒绝该条件将导致分手。

25.original: She sent it back with a note saying, the usual marriage clause, for better or for worse,
25.b1: She sent it back with a note saying that traditional marriage vows,
25.cn: 她把文件退了回来，并附上一张纸条说，传统的婚姻誓言，
25.ai_notes: 'marriage clause': a specific condition or rule within a marriage contract. 'for better or for worse': a traditional phrase from wedding vows meaning to stay together regardless of life's difficulties.
25.remarks:
'marriage clause': 婚前协议中的具体条件或规则。
'for better or for worse': 婚礼誓词中的传统短语，意为无论生活多么艰难都共同面对。

26.original: is good enough for everyone else, and it's good enough for me.
26.b1: which promise to stay together through good and bad times, are enough.
26.cn: 无论好坏都相守，这对别人来说足够了，对我来说也足够了。
26.ai_notes: 'good enough': acceptable or sufficient for a particular purpose.
26.remarks: 'good enough': 对于特定目的来说是可以接受或足够的。

27.ai_notes: 'the whole thing is off': an idiom meaning a plan or event has been cancelled.
27.remarks: 'the whole thing is off': 习语，意为计划或事件已被取消。

28.original: As Jian was relating his experience to me, he said worriedly, now what was so wrong in writing out this agreement?
28.b1: Jian asked me why it was wrong to write down an agreement.
28.cn: 当Jian向我讲述他的经历时，他担忧地问，写下这份协议到底有什么错？
28.ai_notes: 'relating his experience': telling a story or explaining what happened to him.
28.remarks: 'relating his experience': 讲述故事或解释发生在他身上的事情。

29.original: After all, marriage is a big step.
29.b1: After all, marriage is a very important decision.
29.ai_notes: 'big step': a major life decision or a significant change.
29.remarks: 'big step': 重大的人生决定或显著的改变。

30.ai_notes: 'you can't be too careful': an idiom meaning it is impossible to be overly cautious; being careful is always good.
30.remarks: 'you can't be too careful': 习语，意为谨慎永远不为过；小心总是有好处的。

31.ai_notes: 'Jian was wrong': a direct statement used to correct the previous assumption.
31.remarks: 'Jian was wrong': 用于纠正先前假设的直接陈述。

32.original: You can be too careful, too cautious.
32.b1: You can be too careful and too cautious.
32.cn: 人可能会过于谨慎、过于小心。
32.ai_notes: 'too cautious': being so careful that it prevents you from taking necessary risks or living normally.
32.remarks: 'too cautious': 过分谨慎，以至于阻碍了必要的冒险或正常生活。

33.original: Not only in planning a marriage, but in planning anything in the world where things get done.
33.b1: This is true for marriage and for everything else in life.
33.cn: 不仅是在筹划婚姻时，在做任何需要完成的事情时都是如此。
33.ai_notes: 'where things get done': in any situation where you want to achieve a goal.
33.remarks: 'where things get done': 在任何你想达成目标的场合。

34.original: The standards can be too high.
34.b1: Sometimes your standards are too high.
34.ai_notes: 'standards': the level of quality or behavior that someone expects.
34.remarks: 'standards': 某人期望的质量或行为水平。

35.original: Jian's approach to marriage was very much like his approach to his work, his savings, his friendships, everything else.
35.b1: Jian treated marriage the same way he treated his work and friendships.
35.cn: Jian对待婚姻的方式，和他对待工作、储蓄、友谊以及其他一切事物的方式如出一辙。
35.ai_notes: 'as his approach to': the way someone handles or deals with a situation.
35.remarks: 'as his approach to': 某人处理或应对某种情况的方式。

36.original: The test of a successful person is not an ability to eliminate all problems before they arise,
36.b1: Being successful doesn't mean you avoid all problems.
36.cn: 成功人士的考验不在于能否在问题出现前就消除它们，
36.ai_notes: 'eliminate': to remove or get rid of something completely.

37.original: but to meet and work out difficulties when they do arise.
37.b1: It means you can solve problems when they happen.
37.cn: 而在于当困难出现时，能够去面对并解决它们。
37.ai_notes: 'work out': to solve a problem or find a way to handle a difficulty.
37.remarks: 'work out': 解决问题或找到处理困难的方法。

38.original: We must be willing to make an intelligent compromise with perfection, lest we wait forever before taking action.
38.b1: We must accept that nothing is perfect, or we will never do anything.
38.cn: 我们必须愿意在追求完美时做出理性的妥协，否则我们将永远无法采取行动。
38.ai_notes: 'compromise': to accept something less than perfect to reach an agreement. 'lest': a formal word meaning 'to avoid the risk of'.
38.remarks:
'compromise': 为了达成协议而接受不那么完美的结果。
'lest': 正式用词，意为“以免，为了避免……的风险”。

39.original: It's still good advice to cross bridges as we come to them.
39.b1: It is better to solve problems only when they actually happen.
39.cn: 船到桥头自然直，这依然是很好的建议。
39.ai_notes: 'cross bridges as we come to them': an idiom meaning to deal with problems only when they actually happen, rather than worrying about them in advance.
39.remarks: 'cross bridges as we come to them': 习语，意为问题真正发生时再去处理，而不是提前担忧。

40.ai_notes: 'Case number two': the second example in a series of studies.
40.remarks: 'Case number two': 系列研究中的第二个案例。
